item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It has no identifying markings save for a printed label on the inside collar reading 'Dr. E████'

Dr. Eggman?

For real though, this was actually a really interesting idea. The thought of permanently seeing countless numbers of germs crawling on pretty much everything would be pretty agitating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It should be "psychosis", not "psychopathy": psychopathy is a mental disorder characterised by a lack of concern and empathy towards other people, also called sociopathy. Psychosis is abnormal cognition i.e. hallucinations or bizarre ideas or beliefs.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded: Is Cursed a good crosslink?

item.title: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded:
edit: edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show
- Hidet

To link to another article with anomalous sterility, I propose the following edit:

It thus remains sterile save in the immediate period after contact with a colonised surface.

to

It thus remains sterile save in the immediate period after contact with a colonised surface.1

Footnotes
1. While no direct link is suspected between the two objects, SCP-791's anomalous sterility manifests in a similar manner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im curious, what would happen if a piece of cloth were cut off of the coat, and said cloth was Incorporated into a non anomalous piece of clothing? Would the effect of the lab coat transfer to the new cloth piece, or would its anomalous ability no longer manifest? overall, I like this one, its somewhat simple yet well done. I mainly like how any "danger" from this SCP comes not from the SCP itself, but from people who have been affected by the SCP for a long time. It makes sense that they might become dangerous after constantly seeing bacteria with such precision for so long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DrBleep, the shirt.

item.title: Ha.
item.content:encoded:
As soon as I saw "Suspiciously Clean Coat", I had to know. Very direct in what it does, almost brutally so. You also evoke fun visuals here- no sarcasm!

Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At most a very interesting SCP made to be Keter class only for germaphobes lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What I ended up thinking was: "Do not ever expose any test subject to a child or group of children."

I mean, seriously - I can already pretty much see the accumulation of germs on the little buggers darlings already.

+1 for the squirming sounds (ick) and the idea that maybe the doctor was onto something …

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I noticed this as well. I think it's meant to be 10225A1 in the two instances.
In any case, interesting idea with good execution. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why is it that every time I see the name of this SCP after opening the list page, I first read it as Suspiciously Clean Goat? Every. Bleeding. Time.

…oh, the article. Good one! After some internal deliberation, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's probably too late to ask, but: In two places, the D-class subject in Addendum 1022-2&3 is labeled "D-10225A1" and in two others, "D-10225A3". At least I think it's supposed to be the same one. Which should it be?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So this is a squeaky clean jacket that would forever ruin a spoonful of delicious yoghurt. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll be honest- this is the first SCP I've read in a long, long time that sort of made me scream internally. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was almost expecting to see a "Doctor's Orders" label on it. Nice and creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, well-written as verrry unnerving. I think the 1022 curse is finally broken. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it bad i'd kinda want this one?

Though.. would be crap given I need glasses… meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thought this was a good idea and I couldn't resist having someone feel germs inside their body, no matter how silly it may sound.

I've made some slight changes to tone down the 'makes you crazy lol' aspect (though I think it would inevitably cause this, so some of it stays) and put in some more effects to fill the gap.

Let me know if it went in the right or the wrong direction!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why? Unless you're arguing that the SCP's mental effects are expected/'normal' and hence not a sign of mental illness, which would be a bit of a stretch, I don't see what's wrong with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Psychopathology is the wrong word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was 'good, but I'm feeling stingy with my upvotes today' until the last line. The squirming sounds sold it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of the manga/anime Moyashimon, where the main character was born with ability to see microbes as anthropomorphic macroscopic versions of their usual selves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think that being able to permanently see big germs floating around is quite awful in itself, since it would basically give the victims the impression that they are permanently walking in a swarm of disgusting little creatures. For that I could have done even without the induced paranoia (which by the way would make the ending more disquieting as there seem to be no reason for the doctor to snap).

Otherwise, maybe having the victims becoming able to feel the germs as well could be a creepy enough alternative…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This vaguely reminds me of the "GERMS!" episode of Invader Zim. Also, this: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tqOVYpkZ0qs

item.title: Germs...
item.content:encoded: Darn it… now I have Weird Al Yankovic's "Germs" runnign through my head on a loop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's fair, I thought the main problem with this was that the result was really obvious - but it was so obvious I couldn't think of anything which would make more sense to put in. If anybody could think of an interesting twist that would be good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *slow clap*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While reading the part where it describes the effects of it giving you microscopic sight and seeing germs for bigger than they actually are, I just knew that the next bit would cause them to become overly paranoid about germs.
So it's a downvote from me because I found it rather boring and predictable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Both British & USA spellings are acceptable on the site. Make sure words are actually misspelled and not just alternate spelling convention before correcting.

Edit: I.e. go back and change that spelling back; I don't know where you did it and don't particularly care to trawl through for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This appeals to me as a germaphobe. I'd love a constantly sterile lab coat, but I would probably kill myself knowing just how many microbiotic organisms there are around me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, upvoted.

I fixed some minor spelling "theorised" -> "theorized" and some grammar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, especially since I wondered at the end if the affected Doctor would not be right actually…
Also, good use of the creepiness already existing in our world. Those microscopic organisms are ugly when looked in a microscope. Being able to see them all the time…
